revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:Uhō-dōji]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

雨宝童子は、天照坐皇大神の化現である。
頭上に五輪塔を掲げ、右手に金剛宝棒を持ち、左手に如意宝珠を持っている。
天照坐皇大神が日向に下生した時のお姿と伝わる。又、大日如来の化身という説もある。
金剛証寺・長谷寺に祀られている。
http://hasedera.or.jp/history/statue.html
http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm

頭上に五輪塔を掲げ、右手に[[金剛宝棒]]を持ち、左手に[[如意宝珠]]を持っている。
天照坐皇大神が日向に下生した時の姿と伝わる。又、[[大日如来]]の化身という説もある。
[[金剛證寺]]・[[長谷寺]]に祀られている。
== 外部リンク == * http://hasedera.or.jp/history/statue.html * http://www.iseshimaskyline.com/kongoushouji.htm
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:うほうとうし}} [[Category:権現]] [[Category:天津神]]

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = 平安時代の作である木造雨宝童子立像（三重県伊勢市 [[金剛證寺]]所蔵、[[重要文化財]]） | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子画像.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = 室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画（山梨県北杜市 [[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]所蔵） }} '''雨宝童子'''（うほうどうじ）は、[[両部神道]]の神である<ref name="大辞泉">「雨宝童子」『大辞泉』小学館</ref><ref name="大辞林">「雨宝童子」『大辞林』三省堂</ref><ref name="日本国語大辞典">「雨宝童子」『日本国語大辞典』小学館</ref>。[[天照大神|天照坐皇大神]]が日向に下生した際の姿とされ、[[大日如来]]が化現した姿とされることもある<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 特徴 == 右手に金剛宝棒を持ち、左手に[[如意宝珠]]を持ち、頭上に[[五輪塔]]を掲げる<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 文化財 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]にある[[臨済宗南禅寺派]]の[[金剛證寺]]には「木造雨宝童子立像」があり、1912年（明治45年）2月8日には国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された<ref name="金剛證寺">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html 木造雨宝童子立像] 伊勢市</ref>。寺伝によると天長2年（825年）に[[弘法大師]]（空海）によって彫出されたものとされるが、仏像のつくりなどからは[[平安時代]]後期の作と考えられている<ref name="金剛證寺"/>。
[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある[[真言宗豊山派]]の[[長谷寺]]には本尊の左側に「雨宝童子立像」があり、国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="長谷寺">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 あべのハルカス美術館「長谷寺の名宝と十一面観音の信仰」展] 長谷寺、2016年1月10日</ref>。天文7年（1538年）に大仏師の運宗等が造立したものとされる<ref name="長谷寺"/>。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:うほうとうし}} [[Category:権現]] [[Category:天津神]]

[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある[[真言宗豊山派]]の[[長谷寺]]には本尊の左側に「雨宝童子立像」があり、国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="長谷寺">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 あべのハルカス美術館「長谷寺の名宝と十一面観音の信仰」展] 長谷寺、2016年1月10日</ref>。天文7年（1538年）に大仏師の運宗等が造立したものとされる<ref name="長谷寺"/>。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。

== 外部リンク == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 雨宝童子について知りたい] レファレンス協同データベース
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:うほうとうし}} [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:権現]] [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:天津神]]

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = 平安時代の作である木造雨宝童子立像（三重県伊勢市 [[金剛證寺]]所蔵、[[重要文化財]]） | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子画像.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = 室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画（山梨県北杜市 [[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]所蔵） }} '''雨宝童子'''（うほうどうじ）は、[[両部神道]]の神である<ref name="大辞泉">「雨宝童子」『大辞泉』小学館</ref><ref name="大辞林">「雨宝童子」『大辞林』三省堂</ref><ref name="日本国語大辞典">「雨宝童子」『日本国語大辞典』小学館</ref>。[[天照大神|天照坐皇大神]]が日向に下生した際の姿とされ、[[大日如来]]が化現した姿とされることもある<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 特徴 == 右手に金剛宝棒を持ち、左手に[[如意宝珠]]を持ち、頭上に[[五輪塔]]を掲げる<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 文化財 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]にある[[臨済宗南禅寺派]]の[[金剛證寺]]には「木造雨宝童子立像」があり、1912年（明治45年）2月8日には国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された<ref name="金剛證寺">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html 木造雨宝童子立像] 伊勢市</ref>。寺伝によると天長2年（825年）に[[弘法大師]]（空海）によって彫出されたものとされるが、仏像のつくりなどからは[[平安時代]]後期の作と考えられている<ref name="金剛證寺"/>。
[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある[[真言宗豊山派]]の[[長谷寺]]には本尊の左側に「雨宝童子立像」があり、国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="長谷寺">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 あべのハルカス美術館「長谷寺の名宝と十一面観音の信仰」展] 長谷寺、2016年1月10日</ref>。天文7年（1538年）に大仏師の運宗等が造立したものとされる<ref name="長谷寺"/>。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = 平安時代の作である木造雨宝童子立像（三重県伊勢市 [[金剛證寺]]所蔵、[[重要文化財]]） | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子画像.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = 室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画（山梨県北杜市 [[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]所蔵） }} '''雨宝童子'''（うほうどうじ）は、[[両部神道]]の神である<ref name="大辞泉">「雨宝童子」『大辞泉』小学館</ref><ref name="大辞林">「雨宝童子」『大辞林』三省堂</ref><ref name="日本国語大辞典">「雨宝童子」『日本国語大辞典』小学館</ref>。[[天照大神|天照坐皇大神]]が日向に下生した際の姿とされ、[[大日如来]]が化現した姿とされることもある<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 特徴 == 右手に金剛宝棒を持ち、左手に[[如意宝珠]]を持ち、頭上に[[五輪塔]]を掲げる<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 文化財 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]にある[[臨済宗南禅寺派]]の[[金剛證寺]]には「木造雨宝童子立像」があり、1912年（明治45年）2月8日には国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された<ref name="金剛證寺">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html 木造雨宝童子立像] 伊勢市</ref>。寺伝によると天長2年（825年）に[[弘法大師]]（空海）によって彫出されたものとされるが、仏像のつくりなどからは[[平安時代]]後期の作と考えられている<ref name="金剛證寺"/>。
[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある[[真言宗豊山派]]の[[長谷寺]]には本尊の左側に「雨宝童子立像」があり、国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="長谷寺">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 あべのハルカス美術館「長谷寺の名宝と十一面観音の信仰」展] 長谷寺、2016年1月10日</ref>。天文7年（1538年）に大仏師の運宗等が造立したものとされる<ref name="長谷寺"/>。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。

{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:うほうとうし}} [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:権現]] [[Category:Gongen]][[Category:天津神]] [[Category:untranslated pages]][[ja:雨宝童子]][[category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]]

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子画像.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 Information on Uhō-dōji], Collaborative Reference Database

It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.

It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|ja|大辞泉}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|ja|大辞林}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihongo Daijiten|ja|日本国語大辞典}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔|1=Gorintō}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|ja|如意宝珠}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|ja|五輪塔|1=Gorintō}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|ja|金剛證寺}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派}} in {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera (Sakurai)|ja|長谷寺}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|ja|真言宗豊山派}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai City|ja|桜井市}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto City, Yamanashi|ja|北杜市}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
{{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = 平安時代の作である木造雨宝童子立像（三重県伊勢市 [[金剛證寺]]所蔵、[[重要文化財]]） | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子画像.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = 室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画（山梨県北杜市 [[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]所蔵） }} '''雨宝童子'''（うほうどうじ）は、[[両部神道]]の神である<ref name="大辞泉">「雨宝童子」『大辞泉』小学館</ref><ref name="大辞林">「雨宝童子」『大辞林』三省堂</ref><ref name="日本国語大辞典">「雨宝童子」『日本国語大辞典』小学館</ref>。[[天照大神|天照坐皇大神]]が日向に下生した際の姿とされ、[[大日如来]]が化現した姿とされることもある<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 特徴 == 右手に金剛宝棒を持ち、左手に[[如意宝珠]]を持ち、頭上に[[五輪塔]]を掲げる<ref name="大辞泉"/><ref name="大辞林"/><ref name="日本国語大辞典"/>。
== 文化財 == [[三重県]][[伊勢市]]にある[[臨済宗南禅寺派]]の[[金剛證寺]]には「木造雨宝童子立像」があり、1912年（明治45年）2月8日には国の[[重要文化財]]に指定された<ref name="金剛證寺">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html 木造雨宝童子立像] 伊勢市</ref>。寺伝によると天長2年（825年）に[[弘法大師]]（空海）によって彫出されたものとされるが、仏像のつくりなどからは[[平安時代]]後期の作と考えられている<ref name="金剛證寺"/>。
[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]にある[[真言宗豊山派]]の[[長谷寺]]には本尊の左側に「雨宝童子立像」があり、国の重要文化財に指定されている<ref name="長谷寺">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 あべのハルカス美術館「長谷寺の名宝と十一面観音の信仰」展] 長谷寺、2016年1月10日</ref>。天文7年（1538年）に大仏師の運宗等が造立したものとされる<ref name="長谷寺"/>。
[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 雨宝童子について知りたい] レファレンス協同データベース
{{Buddhism-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:うほうとうし}} [[Category:権現]] [[Category:天津神]]

[[山梨県]][[北杜市]]の[[泉龍寺 (北杜市)|泉龍寺]]には室町時代の作とされる雨宝童子垂迹画がある<ref>きたむらひろし『高根の史跡めぐり』きたむらひろし、2012年、p.15</ref>。泉龍寺の山号は雨宝山である。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|qq=|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|qq=|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|qq=|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 Information on Uhō-dōji], Collaborative Reference Database

In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijisen|Daijisen]], Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Daijirin|Daijirin]], Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," [[:en:Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|Nihongo Daijiten]], Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of [[:en:Vairocana|Vairocana]]<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a [[:en:Cintamani|Cintamani]] in the left hand, with a [[:en:Gorintō|Gorintō]] atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In [[:en:Kongōshō-ji|Kongōshō-ji]], a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In [[:en:Hase-dera|Hase-dera (Sakurai)]], a [[:en:Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha]] temple in [[:en:Sakurai, Nara|Sakurai City]], Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in [[:en:Hokuto, Yamanashi|Hokuto City, Yamanashi]], there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48759455]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:14, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|en|Kongōshō-ji|WD=Q3198670|lt=Kongōshō-ji}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|en|Daijirin|WD=Q5209149|lt=Daijirin}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|en|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|WD=Q4093013|lt=Nihongo Daijiten}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana}}<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|en|Cintamani|WD=Q2963837|lt=Cintamani}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|en|Gorintō|WD=Q304906|lt=Gorintō}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|en|Kongōshō-ji|WD=Q3198670|lt=Kongōshō-ji}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hase-dera (Sakurai)}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|WD=Q3077351|lt=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City, Yamanashi}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 Information on Uhō-dōji], Collaborative Reference Database

It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana}}<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|en|Cintamani|WD=Q2963837|lt=Cintamani}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|en|Gorintō|WD=Q304906|lt=Gorintō}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|en|Kongōshō-ji|WD=Q3198670|lt=Kongōshō-ji}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hase-dera (Sakurai)}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|WD=Q3077351|lt=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City, Yamanashi}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Uhō Dōji]][[ja:雨宝童子]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:雨宝童子]] {{Infobox deity|image=File:Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg}} {{multiple image | align = right | direction = horizontal | width = | image1 = Statue of Uhō-dōji at Kongōshō-ji.jpg | width1 = 186 | alt1 = | caption1 = A wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji from the Heian period, housed at {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|en|Kongōshō-ji|WD=Q3198670|lt=Kongōshō-ji|1=Kongōshō-ji}}, Ise City, Mie Prefecture (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | image2 = Hokuto-shi, Senryu-ji,泉龍寺雨寶童子File.jpg | width2 = 180 | alt2 = | caption2 = Uhō-dōji suijaku painting from the Muromachi period, housed at {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji Temple, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, Hokuto City, Yamanashi Prefecture }} '''Uhō-dōji''' (雨宝童子) is a deity in [[:en:Ryōbu Shintō|Ryōbu Shintō]]<ref name="Daijisen">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijisen|en|Daijisen|WD=Q5209153|lt=Daijisen|1=Daijisen}}, Shogakukan</ref><ref name="Daijirin">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Daijirin|en|Daijirin|WD=Q5209149|lt=Daijirin|1=Daijirin}}, Sanseido</ref><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten">"Uhō-dōji," {{ill|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|en|Nihon Kokugo Daijiten|WD=Q4093013|lt=Nihongo Daijiten|1=Nihon Kokugo Daijiten}}, Shogakukan</ref>. It is said to be the form of [[Amaterasu]] when descending to Hyūga and is also considered an incarnation of {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Vairocana|1=Vairocana}}<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Characteristics == Uhō-dōji is depicted holding a {{ill|Vajra staff|ja|金剛宝棒|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vajra staff|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the right hand and a {{ill|Cintamani|en|Cintamani|WD=Q2963837|lt=Cintamani|1=Cintamani}} in the left hand, with a {{ill|Gorintō|en|Gorintō|WD=Q304906|lt=Gorintō|1=Gorintō}} atop the head<ref name="Daijisen"/><ref name="Daijirin"/><ref name="Nihongo Daijiten"/>.
== Cultural Properties == In {{ill|Kongōshō-ji|en|Kongōshō-ji|WD=Q3198670|lt=Kongōshō-ji|1=Kongōshō-ji}}, a temple of the {{ill|Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|ja|臨済宗南禅寺派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rinzai sect Nanzen-ji school|1=Nanzen-ji school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q126509708}} in {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise City|1=Ise, Mie}}, Mie Prefecture, there is a "wooden standing statue of Uhō-dōji," designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} on February 8, 1912 (Meiji 45)<ref name="Kongōshō-ji">[https://www.city.ise.mie.jp/cul_spo_edu/culture/bunkazai_shiseki/bunkazai/chokoku/1009576.html Wooden Standing Statue of Uhō-dōji], Ise City</ref>. According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hase-dera (Sakurai)|1=Hase-dera}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|WD=Q3077351|lt=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>. It is believed to have been created in 1538 (Tenbun 7) by the renowned sculptor Unshū<ref name="Hase-dera"/>.
In {{ill|Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|ja|泉龍寺 (北杜市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senryū-ji (Hokuto City)|1=Senryu-ji Temple, Hokuto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q118512944}}, a temple in {{ill|Hokuto, Yamanashi|en|Hokuto, Yamanashi|WD=Q825272|lt=Hokuto City, Yamanashi|1=Hokuto, Yamanashi}}, there is a suijaku painting of Uhō-dōji from the Muromachi period<ref>Kitamura Hiroshi, ''Takane Historical Sites Guide'', 2012, p.15</ref>. The temple's mountain name (''sangō'') is Rain Treasure Mountain (雨宝山).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://crd.ndl.go.jp/reference/modules/d3ndlcrdentry/index.php?page=ref_view&id=1000103013 Information on Uhō-dōji], Collaborative Reference Database

According to temple tradition, it was carved by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} in 825 (Tenchō 2). However, the style suggests it dates to the late Heian period<ref name="Kongōshō-ji"/>.
In {{ill|Hase-dera|en|Hase-dera|WD=Q1457787|lt=Hase-dera (Sakurai)|1=Hase-dera}}, a {{ill|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|en|Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|WD=Q3077351|lt=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha|1=Shingon-shū Buzan-ha}} temple in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|WD=Q127026|lt=Sakurai City|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, there is another "standing statue of Uhō-dōji" to the left of the main deity, also designated as an Important Cultural Property<ref name="Hase-dera">[https://www.hasedera.or.jp/promotion/1/blog_detail.html?key=entry&value=183 Hase-dera Exhibition at Abeno Harukas Museum], Hase-dera, January 10, 2016</ref>.
